title: achillea rupestris subsp. lucana
intro: Achillea rupestris subsp. lucana, also known as a subspecies of the mountain yarrow plant, is a perennial herb native to Italy and parts of the Mediterranean region. It possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Achillea rupestris subsp. lucana are said to offer various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. This plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried flowers for use in herbal remedies.
